mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of declining INDPRO months or rolling Rank IC falls below -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month deceleration in the consumer price index below 0.1 percent signals easing inflationary pressures, allowing equity markets to reprice risk premiums lower and generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of deceleration events or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with payroll growth (p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth decelerates while retail sales simultaneously contract, the divergence indicates a breakdown in consumer demand transmission, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth < 2.0% and RSAFS MoM < 0%
failure_condition: 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered quarters or the interaction effect fails to improve model log-likelihood significantly.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a five-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of recovery-phase production increases or regime-specific IC drops below 0.01.
